## Comprehensive Repository Exploration Report

I have completed a thorough exploration of the RailOps application repository. Below is the detailed content and structure:

---

## 1. README.md (Full Content - 221 lines)

**File:** `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/README.md`

This is an 8,965-byte document that contains:

- **Architecture Overview**: Vue 3 + Pinia + Router frontend; Koa Node.js REST API; MySQL 8 database; Docker Compose orchestration
- **Key Features**: Trip search with fuzzy matching, schedule versioning, inventory management, bcrypt security, audit trails, backup/recovery, data quality validation
- **Quick Start Instructions**: TLS cert generation, Docker Compose setup with HTTPS on port 3443 and frontend on port 8443
- **Manual Setup**: Full non-Docker instructions with environment variable configuration
- **Default Credentials**: admin/admin123 (Platform Operations), host1/host123 (Host role)
- **Environment Variables**: MYSQL_DATABASE, DB_HOST, DB_PASSWORD, SESSION_SECRET, TLS_CERT_PATH, TLS_KEY_PATH, BACKUP_PATH
- **Scripts Inventory** (7 scripts):
  - `scripts/generate-certs.sh` - Generate self-signed TLS certs
  - `scripts/seed-dev.sh` - Run migrations and seed data
  - `scripts/backup.sh` - Full MySQL backup
  - `scripts/restore-drill.sh` - Restore backup to scratch DB
  - `run_tests.sh` - Run all unit, frontend, and API tests
  - `scripts/benchmark-search.sh` - Measure hot-search response time
- **Test Inventory** (3 categories):
  - **Unit tests** (50 tests): Fuzzy matching, validators, masking, auth middleware
  - **Frontend tests**: Vue stores, components, router, page-level flows (Vitest)
  - **API tests** (94 tests): Auth, trips, schedules, inventory, stations, users, backups, data quality, authorization (requires running backend)
- **Performance Target**: Cached hot searches return in under 200ms
- **Project Structure**: Detailed directory breakdown showing frontend/, backend/, unit_tests/, API_tests/, scripts/, docs/

---

## 2. docs/design.md (Full Content - 907 lines)

**File:** `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/docs/design.md`

### Key Sections:

**System Overview**:
- Technology Stack: Vue 3, Pinia, Vue Router, IndexedDB (frontend); Node.js, Koa, knex (backend); MySQL 8; bcrypt auth; Docker Compose with TLS
- **Three Roles**: Guest (read-only search), Host (station-scoped schedule/inventory), Platform Operations (approvals, cross-site)
- **Core Principles**: Offline-first (zero external calls), TLS everywhere, full auditability

**Architecture (Section 2)**:
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router (history mode), Pinia stores (authStore, scheduleStore, inventoryStore, searchStore), IndexedDB hot-search cache, fetch API client with Bearer token auth
- **Backend**: Koa application with middleware stack (tlsEnforce, requestId, rateLimiter, sessionAuth, roleGuard, errorHandler), organized routes (/api/auth, /api/schedules, /api/inventory, /api/stations, /api/admin, /api/search), business services, knex-backed data access
- **Database**: MySQL 8 on-site with binary logging enabled for point-in-time recovery
- **Docker Compose**: Frontend (nginx + Vue SPA), backend (Node.js Koa), db (MySQL 8), backup (nightly dump + binlog sidecar)

**Data Model (Section 3)** - Comprehensive database schema with 10 subsections:

3.1 **Identity & Access**: users (id, username, password_hash[bcrypt], phone, role_id, is_active, enrolled_at), roles (guest, host, platform_ops), permissions (role_id, action, scope), user_station_scopes (user_id, station_id for host scoping)

3.2 **Session & Device Security**: sessions (id, user_id, token_hash[SHA-256], device_fingerprint, expires_at[30 days], last_active_at, is_revoked), trusted_devices, recovery_codes (single-use bcrypt hashes), login_attempts, lockouts (15 min for first, admin reset on 3rd within 24hr)

3.3 **Stations**: stations (id, code, name, normalized_name, timezone, is_active), station_aliases (for fuzzy matching)

3.4 **Trainsets**: trainsets (id, code, description, total_seats, is_active)

3.5 **Schedules & Versioning**: schedules (id, train_number, trainset_id, origin_station, dest_station, active_version), schedule_versions (id, schedule_id, version_number, status[draft/pending_approval/published/archived], effective_from, effective_until), schedule_stops (version_id, station_id, stop_sequence, arrival_time, departure_time, dwell_minutes), seat_classes (version_id, name, capacity[1-500], price[$1-$999])

3.6 **Approval & Rollback**: approval_requests (version_id, requested_by, reviewed_by, status, comment[mandatory on reject]), rollback_history (schedule_id, from_version_id, to_version_id, source_version_id, rolled_back_by, reason)

3.7 **Inventory**: inventory_items (id, station_id, sku, description, unit_of_measure, tracking_mode[none/batch/serial], reorder_point, unit_cost, is_active), inventory_balances (id, item_id, on_hand[cached], as_of), inventory_movements (id, item_id, type[receiving/shipping/material_return/customer_return/adjustment], quantity[positive inbound/negative outbound], reference_number, performed_by), stock_counts (id, station_id, status[in_progress/completed/cancelled]), count_lines (count_id, item_id, expected_qty, counted_qty, variance_qty, variance_cost, resolved), batch_records, serial_records

3.8 **Backup & Recovery**: backups (id, type[full/incremental], file_path, file_size, checksum[SHA-256], status[running/completed/failed]), restore_drills (backup_id, target_schema, success, row_count_match, report[JSON], performed_by)

3.9 **Data Quality**: data_quality_issues (table_name, record_id, field_name, severity[critical/high/medium/low], rule_name, status[open/in_progress/resolved/wont_fix]), data_quality_reports (report_date, total_issues, critical_count, pass_rate, report_payload[JSON])

3.10 **Audit Logs**: audit_logs (user_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_values[JSON], new_values[JSON], ip_address, session_id, created_at) - append-only with database trigger enforcement

**Security Design (Section 4)**:
- **Authentication**: bcrypt cost factor 12, no SSO/LDAP/OAuth
- **Session Management**: 256-bit random tokens (SHA-256 hash in DB), 8-hour inactivity timeout, 30-day hard expiry, 2 max sessions per user (override via exception), HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=strict cookies
- **Device Fingerprinting**: Browser user-agent + OS + localStorage machine UUID; trusted devices vs. new device challenge with recovery codes
- **Recovery Codes**: 10 single-use alphanumeric codes at enrollment, bcrypt hashed, displayed once and never again, warning at <3 remaining
- **Progressive Lockout**: 5 failures in 10min = 15min lock; 3 lockouts in 24hr = admin reset required
- **Rate Limiting**: `/api/auth/login` limited to 10 requests (bucket) with 1 refill/3 sec per IP
- **Row-Level Scope**: Hosts filtered by `user_station_scopes` (service layer enforcement); Platform Ops see all; Guests see published only
- **Sensitive Field Masking**: user.phone masked to last 4 digits for Host/Guest, full value for Platform Ops
- **TLS Configuration**: Mandatory TLS 1.2+, admin provides cert/key, dev generates self-signed, tlsEnforce middleware rejects HTTP

**Guest Search Design (Section 5)**:
- **Search Filters**: Origin (station autocomplete), Destination, Date (MM/DD/YYYY), Seat class (multi-select)
- **Sort Options**: Departure time (default), trip duration, price (lowest seat class)
- **Fuzzy Station Matching**: 3-tier strategy — exact match on normalized_name/aliases, prefix match via LIKE, Levenshtein distance ≤2 for typos
- **Hot Search Cache (IndexedDB)**: Key = sorted query params (origin|destination|date|seatClasses|sort), TTL = 1 hour, max 200 entries with LRU eviction, If-Modified-Since polling
- **Empty State**: "No matches found. Try nearby dates" with clickable ±3 day links
- **Performance Targets**: Cache hit <50ms, cache miss warm server <200ms, cold start <500ms
- **Backend Optimization**: Composite index on `(origin_station, dest_station, status, effective_from)` in schedule_versions

**Schedule Management (Section 6)**:
- **Draft Workspace**: Max 1 draft version per schedule, private to creating Host, editable stops/times/seat classes/trainsets
- **Pre-Publish Validation**: ≥3 stops (origin + dest + 1), time sequence (arrival ≥ departure, arrival > prev departure), capacity 1-500, price $1-$999, trainset availability (no overlapping published)
- **Version Comparison**: Side-by-side diff showing additions (green), removals (red), modifications (amber), summary stats (change count, affected stops, price delta)
- **Approval Flow**: Host submits → Platform Ops approves (status→published, active_version updated, prev archived) OR rejects (status→draft, mandatory comment, audit logged); Host cannot approve own submission
- **Rollback**: Clone historical version to new row with next version number, set status=published, archive prev, create rollback_history, audit log entry; never deletes data

**Inventory Design (Section 7)**:
- **Movement Types**: receiving (inbound), shipping (outbound), material_return (inbound), customer_return (inbound), stock_count, adjustment (exempt from no-negative rule)
- **Ledger-Based Balances**: Balance = SUM(movements.quantity) WHERE item_id AND created_at ≤ t; inventory_balances is materialized cache recomputed after each movement + every 15min
- **No-Negative-On-Hand Rule**: Reject movements <0 except adjustments; transactional lock on inventory_balances to prevent race conditions
- **Reorder Alerts**: When on_hand ≤ reorder_point (default 20, configurable), surface in dashboard + dedicated panel
- **Variance Alerts**: >2% quantity variance OR >$50 cost variance requires review; reviewer accepts (creates adjustment) or recounts
- **Batch & Serial Tracking**: tracking_mode = none/batch/serial (immutable after first movement); batch_records (batch_number, expiry_date, quantity); serial_records (serial_number, status[in_stock/shipped/returned/disposed])

**Backup & Recovery (Section 8)**:
- **Schedule**: Full nightly at 02:00 via mysqldump (--single-transaction --routines --triggers), incremental every 15min via binlog
- **Target**: Admin-designated removable drive path (BACKUP_TARGET_PATH env var); timestamp-named files, gzip compression, SHA-256 checksums in DB
- **Retention**: Full 90 days, binlogs 30 days; nightly pruning by sidecar
- **Quarterly Drills**: Select backup → restore to scratch schema → compare row counts → create restore_drills record → drop scratch schema; audit logged; Platform Ops warned if none in quarter

**Data Quality & Lineage (Section 9)**:
- **Write-Time Validations**: Completeness (non-null required), uniqueness (user-friendly errors), freshness (timestamps in bounds, e.g., schedules ≤2 years past), referential (FK targets exist, active), domain (ranges: capacity 1-500, price $1-$999, etc.)
- **Daily Quality Reports**: 05:00 job scans all tables for missing required fields, orphaned FKs, stale drafts (>30 days), duplicate detection, inventory cache drift; writes to data_quality_reports + data_quality_issues
- **Issue Tracking**: Severity (critical/high/medium/low), owner (nullable, required for critical/high), due_date, status (open/in_progress/resolved/wont_fix), resolution (free-text documentation)
- **Point-in-Time Diff/Replay**: Using audit_logs JSON snapshots, view changes between two timestamps on a record, or chronologically step through mutations (read-only, Platform Ops only)
- **Corrective Action**: Resolver documents what was wrong, what was fixed, process change needed; stored in resolution field; included in quarterly summary

**Appendix A - Key API Endpoints** (11 endpoints listed with auth, role requirements)

**Appendix B - Environment Variables** (partial list shown)

---

## 3. docs/api-spec.md (Full Content - 3,702 lines)

**File:** `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/docs/api-spec.md`

**Version:** 1.0.0 | **Transport:** HTTPS (TLS) | **Server:** Koa | **Base URL:** `https://<local-host>:<port>/api` | **Content-Type:** application/json

### Conventions:
- **Roles**: guest (unauthenticated, read-only search), host (station-scoped), platform_ops (full access)
- **Standard Success Response**: `{ "success": true, "data": { ... } }` or with pagination `{ results: [...], total: 142, page: 1, pageSize: 25 }`
- **Standard Error Response**: `{ "success": false, "error": { code: "ERROR_CODE", message: "...", details: {...} } }`
- **Common Error Codes**: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 401 UNAUTHENTICATED, 403 FORBIDDEN, 404 NOT_FOUND, 409 CONFLICT, 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED, 429 RATE_LIMITED, 500 INTERNAL_ERROR
- **Pagination**: `page` (default 1), `pageSize` (default 25, max 100)
- **Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` header
- **Audit Logging**: Every POST/PATCH/DELETE recorded with actor, action, target, timestamp, before/after snapshot

### Authentication & Sessions:

**POST /api/auth/login**
- Request: username, password, deviceFingerprint (all required)
- Success (200): token, expiresAt, user (id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds)
- Errors: 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS, 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED (with lockedUntil), 403 DEVICE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED (with sessionToken), 401 ENROLLMENT_REQUIRED
- Notable: Failed attempts counted; 5 failures in 10min = 15min lockout (423); unknown device → DEVICE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED (403); no recovery codes → ENROLLMENT_REQUIRED (401); HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite cookie + Bearer token; audit logs login success/failure with IP + fingerprint

**POST /api/auth/logout**
- Request: (none, authenticated)
- Success (200): { message: "Session invalidated." }
- Error: 401 UNAUTHENTICATED

**POST /api/auth/verify-device**
- Request: sessionToken, code (8 alphanumeric), deviceFingerprint (all required)
- Success (200): { success: true, token: "...", user: { id, username, display_name, role, phone_last4 } }
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 401 VERIFICATION_FAILED
- Notable: Single-use codes; consumed on success; device added to trusted_devices; pending verification session deleted

**GET /api/auth/me** (✓ Key Response Contract!)
- Required: any authenticated user
- Success (200):
  ```json
  {
    "success": true,
    "data": {
      "id": "usr_001",
      "username": "jdoe",
      "displayName": "Jane Doe",
      "role": "host",
      "assignedStationIds": ["stn_012"],
      "recoveryCodesRemaining": 3,
      "createdAt": "2025-06-15T10:00:00Z",
      "lastLoginAt": "2026-04-03T08:14:22Z"
    }
  }
  ```

**POST /api/auth/recovery-codes**
- Request: (none, authenticated)
- Success (200): { codes: ["A1B2C3D4", ...10 total], generatedAt: "...", warning: "Store these codes securely..." }
- Notable: Replaces all unused codes; audit logged

### Users & Admin:

**GET /api/users**
- Required: platform_ops
- Query: page, pageSize, role, q (substring search), locked (filter)
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/users**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: username (3-64 alphanumeric+underscore, unique), displayName (1-128), password (8-128, complexity), role (host/platform_ops), assignedStationIds[] (optional)
- Success (201): { id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds, locked, createdAt }
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (username exists)

**PATCH /api/users/:id**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: displayName, role, assignedStationIds (all optional)
- Success (200): updated user object
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**GET /api/users/:id/sessions**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { results: [{ sessionId, deviceFingerprint, ipAddress, createdAt, expiresAt, lastActivityAt }], total, page, pageSize }

**DELETE /api/users/:id/sessions/:sessionId**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { message: "Session revoked." }
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**POST /api/users/:id/session-exception**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: maxSessions (2-10), expiresAt (ISO 8601, future), reason (1-500 chars)
- Success (200): { userId, maxSessions, expiresAt, grantedBy, grantedAt }

**POST /api/users/:id/unlock**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { userId, locked: false, unlockedBy, unlockedAt }
- Errors: 404 NOT_FOUND, 409 CONFLICT (not locked)
- Notable: Resets failed-attempt counter; audit logged

**POST /api/users/:id/reset-password**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: newPassword (8-128, complexity)
- Success (200): { userId, mustChangePassword: true, resetBy, resetAt }
- Notable: User required to change on next login; all sessions invalidated; audit logged

### Stations:

**GET /api/stations**
- Query: page, pageSize, q (fuzzy search by name/code/aliases, ranked by match score)
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, code, name, timezone, platforms, coordinates, active, createdAt, updatedAt }], total, page, pageSize }

**GET /api/stations/:id**
- Success (200): station detail with assignedHosts array
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**POST /api/stations**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: code (2-10 alphanumeric uppercase, unique), name (1-200, unique), timezone (IANA), platforms (1-100), coordinates (optional)
- Success (201): created station object
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT

**PATCH /api/stations/:id**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: name, timezone, platforms, coordinates, active (all optional)
- Success (200): updated station object
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

### Trainsets:

**GET /api/trainsets**
- Query: page, pageSize, status (active/maintenance/retired filter)
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, code, name, type[electric/diesel/hybrid], cars, totalSeats, status, homeStationId, createdAt, updatedAt }], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/trainsets**
- Required: host or platform_ops
- Request: code (2-20, unique), name (1-200), type (electric/diesel/hybrid), cars (1-30), totalSeats (1-2000), status (default active), homeStationId (required)
- Success (201): created trainset object
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT

**PATCH /api/trainsets/:id**
- Required: host or platform_ops
- Success (200): updated trainset object

### Trip Search (Guest - Public):

**GET /api/trips/search**
- Query: origin (required), destination (required), date (optional, MM/DD/YYYY), seatClass (optional multi-select), sort (optional: departure/duration/price)
- Success (200): { results: [{ routeName, origin: { stationName, departureAt }, destination: { stationName }, seatClasses: [{ classCode, className, fare }], durationMinutes }] }
- Errors: 400 for missing/invalid params

**GET /api/trips/hot-searches**
- Success (200): { results: [{ origin, destination, searchCount, lastSearched }] }

### Schedules:

**GET /api/schedules**
- Query: page, pageSize, status (draft/pending_approval/published/archived filter)
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

**GET /api/schedules/:id**
- Success (200): schedule detail
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**GET /api/schedules/:id/versions**
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, version_number, status, effective_from, effective_until, created_by, created_at, published_at }], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/schedules**
- Request: route_name (required), trainset_id, stops (array: station_id, departure_at, arrival_at), seat_classes (array: class_code, class_name, capacity, fare)
- Success (201): { id, route_name, trainset_id, status, ... }
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR

**POST /api/schedules/:id/versions/:vid/validate**
- Success (200): { valid: boolean, checks: [{ name, passed, message }] }

**POST /api/schedules/:id/rollback**
- Request: from_version_id, to_version_id, reason
- Success (200): new version object
- Error: 409 CONFLICT (cannot rollback from draft)

### Inventory Items:

**GET /api/inventory/items**
- Query: page, pageSize, station_id (filter)
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, sku, name, station_id, unit_of_measure, on_hand, tracking_mode, reorder_point, unit_cost, is_active }], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/inventory/items**
- Request: sku (required, unique per station), name, station_id (required), unit_of_measure, unit_cost, reorder_point, tracking_mode (none/batch/serial)
- Success (201): created item object
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR

### Inventory Movements:

**POST /api/inventory/movements**
- Request: item_id (required), station_id (required), movement_type (receiving/shipping/material_return/customer_return/adjustment), quantity (required, positive), reference_number, notes
- Success (201): created movement object
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (would go negative)

**GET /api/inventory/movements**
- Query: page, pageSize, item_id (filter), type (filter)
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

### Stock Counts:

**POST /api/inventory/stock-counts**
- Request: station_id (required), notes
- Success (201): { id, station_id, status: "in_progress", started_at, started_by }
- Error: 409 CONFLICT (open count exists)

**PATCH /api/inventory/stock-counts/:id**
- Request: lines (array: { item_id, counted_quantity })
- Success (200): updated count object

**GET /api/inventory/stock-counts**
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

### Backups & Recovery:

**GET /api/backups**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, backup_type[full/incremental], file_path, file_size, checksum, started_at, completed_at, status[running/completed/failed] }], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/backups/run**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: backup_type (full/incremental)
- Success (200/202): backup initiated (sync or async)
- Error: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (already running)

**GET /api/restore-drills**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

### Data Quality:

**GET /api/data-quality/issues**
- Required: platform_ops
- Query: page, pageSize, severity, status, table_name
- Success (200): { results: [...], total, page, pageSize }

**POST /api/data-quality/issues**
- Required: platform_ops
- Request: entity_type, check_type, severity, description
- Success (201): created issue object

**GET /api/data-quality/reports**
- Required: platform_ops
- Success (200): { results: [{ report_date, total_issues, critical_count, tables_scanned, pass_rate, report_payload }], total, page, pageSize }

### Audit:

**GET /api/audit/logs**
- Required: platform_ops
- Query: page, pageSize, action (filter), user_id (filter), table_name (filter), record_id (filter)
- Success (200): { results: [{ id, user_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_values[JSON], new_values[JSON], ip_address, created_at }], total, page, pageSize }

### Test Files List:

# Comprehensive Repository Exploration Report

## 1. README.md (Full Content)

Lines 1-221 - Complete content available above. Key sections:

**Architecture Overview:**
- Frontend: Vue 3 + Pinia + Router
- Backend: Koa (Node 18+), REST API with HTTPS required
- Database: MySQL 8.0
- Runtime: Docker Compose
- TLS mandatory in all operational modes (HTTP only permitted when NODE_ENV=test)
- Frontend dev server: port 8443 | Backend API: https://localhost:3443

**Roles:**
- Guest: Read-only trip search on published schedules
- Host: Schedule management, inventory workflows (station-scoped)
- Platform Operations: Approvals, audit, user admin, backups, cross-site access

**Default Credentials:**
- admin / admin123 (Platform Operations)
- host1 / host123 (Host)

**Test Inventory:**
- Unit tests: 50 tests covering fuzzy matching, validators, masking, auth middleware
- Frontend tests: Vue stores, components, router, page-level flow tests via Vitest
- API tests: 94 tests covering auth, trips, schedules, inventory, stations, users, backups, data quality, authorization

**Key Scripts:**
- `scripts/generate-certs.sh` - Generate self-signed TLS certs
- `run_tests.sh` - Run all unit, frontend, and API tests
- `scripts/benchmark-search.sh` - Verify <200ms hot search performance

## 2. docs/design.md (Full Content - 907 Lines)

**Key Sections with Line Numbers:**

**Lines 1-31: System Overview**
- LAN-only, offline-first application
- Technology: Vue 3, Node.js/Koa, MySQL 8, Docker Compose, TLS everywhere
- Three core principles: offline-first, TLS everywhere, auditability

**Lines 33-93: Architecture**
- Frontend: Vue Router guards, Pinia stores (authStore, scheduleStore, inventoryStore, searchStore), IndexedDB hot-search cache
- Backend: Koa with middleware (tlsEnforce, requestId, rateLimiter, sessionAuth, roleGuard, errorHandler)
- Routes: /api/auth, /api/schedules, /api/inventory, /api/stations, /api/admin, /api/search
- knex query builder (no ORM), bcrypt cost factor 12, 256-bit session tokens
- Docker Compose: frontend (nginx TLS), backend (Node.js), db (MySQL), backup (sidecar)

**Lines 99-500: Data Model (Complete Schema)**

**Identity & Access Tables (Lines 102-142):**
- `users`: id, username, display_name, password_hash, phone, role_id, is_active, enrolled_at, created_at, updated_at
- `roles`: id, name (guest, host, platform_ops)
- `permissions`: id, role_id, action, scope (own_station, cross_site, public)
- `user_station_scopes`: id, user_id, station_id

**Session & Device Security (Lines 145-202):**
- `sessions`: id, user_id, token_hash (SHA-256), device_fingerprint, created_at, last_active_at, expires_at (30 days), is_revoked
- `trusted_devices`: id, user_id, device_fingerprint, label, trusted_at
- `recovery_codes`: id, user_id, code_hash (bcrypt), is_used, used_at, created_at
- `login_attempts`: id, username, ip_address, device_fingerprint, success, attempted_at
- `lockouts`: id, user_id, ip_address, reason, locked_at, expires_at (15 min first, admin reset on 3rd), cleared_by, cleared_at

**Stations (Lines 204-227):**
- `stations`: id, code, name, normalized_name, timezone, is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `station_aliases`: id, station_id, alias, normalized_alias

**Trainsets (Lines 229-240):**
- `trainsets`: id, code, description, total_seats, is_active, created_at, updated_at

**Schedules & Versioning (Lines 242-294):**
- `schedules`: id, train_number, trainset_id, origin_station, dest_station, active_version, created_by, created_at, updated_at
- `schedule_versions`: id, schedule_id, version_number, status (draft, pending_approval, published, archived), effective_from, effective_until, created_by, created_at, published_at, archived_at
- `schedule_stops`: id, version_id, station_id, stop_sequence, arrival_time, departure_time, dwell_minutes
- `seat_classes`: id, version_id, name, capacity (1-500), price ($1.00-$999.00)

**Approval & Rollback (Lines 295-321):**
- `approval_requests`: id, version_id, requested_by (Host), reviewed_by (Platform Ops), status (pending, approved, rejected), comment, requested_at, reviewed_at
- `rollback_history`: id, schedule_id, from_version_id, to_version_id, source_version_id, rolled_back_by, reason, created_at

**Inventory (Lines 323-410):**
- `inventory_items`: id, station_id, sku, description, unit_of_measure, tracking_mode (none, batch, serial), reorder_point, unit_cost, is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `inventory_balances`: id, item_id, on_hand (cached), as_of
- `inventory_movements`: id, item_id, type (receiving, shipping, material_return, customer_return, adjustment), quantity, unit_cost, reference_number, notes, performed_by, created_at
- `stock_counts`: id, station_id, status (in_progress, completed, cancelled), started_by, started_at, completed_at
- `count_lines`: id, count_id, item_id, expected_qty, counted_qty, variance_qty, variance_cost, resolved
- `batch_records`: id, movement_id, batch_number, expiry_date, quantity
- `serial_records`: id, movement_id, serial_number, status (in_stock, shipped, returned, disposed)

**Backup & Recovery (Lines 411-440):**
- `backups`: id, type (full, incremental), file_path, file_size, checksum (SHA-256), started_at, completed_at, status (running, completed, failed), error_message
- `restore_drills`: id, backup_id, target_schema, started_at, completed_at, success, row_count_match, report (JSON), performed_by

**Data Quality (Lines 441-475):**
- `data_quality_issues`: id, table_name, record_id, field_name, severity (critical, high, medium, low), rule_name, description, owner_id, due_date, status (open, in_progress, resolved, wont_fix), resolution, detected_at, resolved_at
- `data_quality_reports`: id, report_date, total_issues, critical_count, high_count, tables_scanned, records_scanned, pass_rate, report_payload (JSON), generated_at

**Audit Logs (Lines 476-493):**
- `audit_logs`: id, user_id (nullable), action, table_name, record_id, old_values (JSON), new_values (JSON), ip_address, session_id, created_at
- Append-only table; no UPDATE or DELETE permitted

**Lines 497-577: Security Design**

**Authentication (Lines 499-502):**
- Local credentials only, bcrypt cost factor 12
- No plaintext storage or transmission, TLS protects in transit

**Session Management (Lines 504-514):**
- Token format: 256-bit cryptographically random, stored as SHA-256 hash
- Inactivity timeout: 8 hours since last authenticated request
- Hard expiry: 30 days from session creation
- Max active sessions: 2 per user (Platform Ops can override)
- Token transport: HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=strict session cookie (Bearer header accepted as fallback)

**Device Fingerprinting (Lines 516-526):**
- Composed of: browser user-agent, OS identifier, machine key (random UUID persisted in localStorage)
- Known device: login proceeds normally
- New device: user challenged with recovery code; on success, device added to trusted_devices

**Recovery Codes (Lines 528-534):**
- Generated at enrollment: 10 single-use alphanumeric codes
- Stored as bcrypt hash
- Displayed once, never shown again
- Warning when < 3 remain

**Progressive Lockout (Lines 536-541):**
- 5 failed attempts within 10 minutes: lock for 15 minutes
- 3 lockouts within 24 hours: lock until admin reset

**Rate Limiting (Lines 545-550):**
- /api/auth/login endpoint: token-bucket algorithm
- Bucket size: 10 requests, refill: 1 per 3 seconds, scoped per IP
- Returns 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header

**Row-Level Data Scope (Lines 553-559):**
- Hosts: See schedules, inventory, movements for assigned stations only
- Platform Ops: No scope filter; cross-site visibility
- Guests: Only published schedule versions through public search

**Sensitive Field Masking (Lines 561-569):**
- `user.phone`: Guest (not visible), Host (last 4 digits ****-**-1234), Platform Ops (full value)
- Applied in service layer response serializer

**TLS Configuration (Lines 571-576):**
- Administrators provide TLS certificate and private key via environment variables or mounted files
- Development: generate self-signed cert
- Backend rejects plain HTTP; tlsEnforce middleware returns 403
- Minimum TLS version: 1.2

**Lines 580-634: Guest Search Design**

**Search Interface (Lines 582-592):**
- Filters: Origin (station autocomplete, required), Destination (required), Date (MM/DD/YYYY, required), Seat class (multi-select, optional)

**Sort Options (Lines 594-596):**
- Departure time (default, ascending)
- Trip duration (ascending)
- Price (ascending, cheapest seat class)

**Fuzzy Station Matching (Lines 601-607):**
- Tier 1: Exact match on normalized_name or normalized_alias
- Tier 2: Prefix match using LIKE 'input%'
- Tier 3: Levenshtein distance (threshold <= 2) for typo tolerance
- Normalization: lowercase, strip accents (NFD), remove punctuation, collapse whitespace

**Hot Search Cache - IndexedDB (Lines 609-616):**
- Cache key: normalized string from sorted query parameters: `origin|destination|date|seatClasses|sort`
- TTL: 1 hour from cache time
- Eviction: LRU when > 200 entries
- Cache invalidation: backend includes Last-Modified header; frontend sends If-Modified-Since; 304 Not Modified means cache valid

**Empty State (Lines 618-624):**
- "No matches found. Try nearby dates."
- Clickable links for +/- 3 days from searched date

**Performance Target (Lines 626-634):**
- Cache hit (IndexedDB): < 50ms
- Cache miss, warm server: < 200ms
- Cold server start: < 500ms
- Backend optimized with composite index on (origin_station, dest_station, status, effective_from)

**Lines 638-707: Schedule Management Design**

**Draft Workspace (Lines 640-648):**
- At most one draft version per schedule
- Drafts private to creating Host until submitted
- Supports: add/reorder/remove stops, set times, define seat classes, assign trainset

**Pre-Publish Validation Checklist (Lines 650-662):**
- Minimum stops: At least 1 intermediate stop (origin + destination + 1)
- Time sequence: departure >= arrival; arrival > previous stop's departure
- Capacity per seat class: 1-500
- Price per seat class: $1.00-$999.00
- Trainset availability: No overlapping published schedule for same time window

**Version Comparison (Lines 664-672):**
- Side-by-side diff: Added stops (green), Removed stops (red), Modified fields (amber)
- Summary: number of changes, affected stops, price delta

**Approval Flow (Lines 674-686):**
- Host creates draft → Host submits (pending_approval) → Platform Ops reviews
- Approve: status=published, schedule.active_version updated, previous version archived
- Reject: status=draft, rejection comment mandatory
- Host cannot approve own submission
- Creates audit log entries

**Rollback (Lines 688-698):**
- Platform Ops can roll back to any previously published version
- Historical version cloned into new schedule_versions row with next version number
- New version status=published, becomes active_version
- Previously active version archived
- rollback_history record created
- Full version history always preserved

**Active Version Display (Lines 700-706):**
- "LIVE" badge, effective-from timestamp, version number, published by

**Lines 709-770: Inventory Design**

**Movement Types (Lines 711-720):**
- receiving (inbound), shipping (outbound), material_return (inbound), customer_return (inbound), stock_count (either), adjustment (either)

**Ledger-Based Balances (Lines 722-740):**
- Computed from movement ledger: Balance(item, t) = SUM(movements.quantity) WHERE item_id = item AND created_at <= t
- inventory_balances is materialized cache, recomputed:
  - After each movement
  - On scheduled reconciliation (every 15 minutes)
- Discrepancy during reconciliation raises data_quality_issue

**No-Negative-On-Hand Rule (Lines 742-740):**
- All movement types except adjustment reject any movement bringing on_hand below zero
- Adjustments exempt to correct discrepancies
- Database transaction locks inventory_balances row to prevent race conditions

**Reorder Alerts (Lines 742-748):**
- Triggered when movement causes on_hand <= reorder_point
- Displayed in inventory dashboard and dedicated "Reorder Alerts" panel
- Default reorder_point: 20 units (configurable per item)

**Variance Alerts (Lines 750-759):**
- Flagged if variance exceeds: Quantity > 2% of expected, OR Cost > $50.00
- Require review before stock count marked complete
- Reviewer can accept (create adjustment movement) or recount

**Batch and Serial Tracking (Lines 761-769):**
- none: No sub-item tracking, quantities only
- batch: Movement includes batch_number and optional expiry_date; useful for consumables
- serial: Each unit has unique serial_number; lifecycle tracked (in_stock, shipped, returned, disposed)
- Tracking mode set at item creation; cannot change after first movement

**Lines 773-810: Backup & Recovery**

**Backup Schedule (Lines 775-780):**
- Full: Nightly at 02:00 via mysqldump with --single-transaction --routines --triggers
- Incremental: Every 15 minutes via MySQL binary log archival

**Backup Target (Lines 782-789):**
- Written to admin-designated removable drive path (BACKUP_TARGET_PATH environment variable)
- Sidecar container mounts as volume
- Named with timestamp: `railops_full_20260403_020000.sql.gz` or `railops_binlog_20260403_021500.bin`
- Compressed with gzip (full only), checksummed with SHA-256

**Retention (Lines 791-795):**
- Full backups: 90 days on removable drive
- Binary logs: 30 days
- Sidecar prunes during nightly run

**Quarterly Restore Drills (Lines 797-807):**
- Select recent full backup
- Restore into temporary scratch schema (e.g., railops_drill_20260403)
- Compare row counts between live and restored
- restore_drills record with comparison results
- Scratch schema dropped after
- Logged in audit trail; Platform Ops reminded if no drill in current quarter

**Lines 811-867: Data Quality & Lineage**

**Write-Time Validations (Lines 813-825):**
- Completeness, Uniqueness, Freshness, Referential, Domain
- Return structured error responses with field-level detail

**Daily Quality Reports (Lines 827-837):**
- Run daily at 05:00
- Scan for: missing required fields, orphaned FKs, stale records, duplicates, inventory balance cache drift
- Results to data_quality_reports and data_quality_issues
- Visible to Platform Ops on admin dashboard

**Issue Tracking (Lines 839-848):**
- Severity: critical, high, medium, low
- Owner: Assigned user (nullable initially, must assign for critical/high)
- Due date: Expected resolution
- Status: open, in_progress, resolved, wont_fix
- Resolution: Free-text documentation

**Point-in-Time Diff and Replay (Lines 850-857):**
- Using audit_logs (stores old/new JSON snapshots)
- Point-in-time diff: select two timestamps, view changes to record
- Replay view: step through mutations chronologically
- Read-only, available to Platform Ops

**Corrective Action Documentation (Lines 859-866):**
- What was wrong, what was done, process changes needed
- Stored in resolution field, included in quarterly summary

**Lines 870-900: Appendix A - Key API Endpoints**

Comprehensive endpoint list with method, path, auth requirement, role

**Lines 892-899: Appendix B - Environment Variables**

Full list of configuration variables with defaults

**Lines 114-316: Authentication & Sessions**

**POST /api/auth/login (Lines 116-172)**
- Required role: none (public)
- Request: username (3-64 chars), password (8-128 chars), deviceFingerprint (non-empty)
- Success 200: { token, expiresAt, user { id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds } }
- Errors:
  - 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS: wrong username or password
  - 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED: after 5 failures in 10 min, locked 15 min
  - 403 DEVICE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED: unrecognised device, includes sessionToken
  - 401 ENROLLMENT_REQUIRED: user has no recovery codes
- Notable: Failed attempts counted; 5 in 10 min = lock; successful login resets counter; HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite cookie set; audit logs login with IP and device fingerprint

**POST /api/auth/logout (Lines 175-200)**
- Required role: any authenticated user
- Success 200: { message: "Session invalidated." }
- Error 401: UNAUTHENTICATED if token missing/expired

**POST /api/auth/verify-device (Lines 203-255)**
- Required role: none (but requires sessionToken from prior login with requireDeviceVerification: true)
- Request: sessionToken, code (8 chars alphanumeric), deviceFingerprint (non-empty)
- Success 200: { success, token, user { id, username, display_name, role, phone_last4 } }
- Errors:
  - 400 VALIDATION_ERROR: missing fields
  - 401 VERIFICATION_FAILED: invalid/expired token or invalid code
- Notable: Each code single-use; consumed on success; device fingerprint stored as trusted; pending verification session deleted

**GET /api/auth/me (Lines 258-282)** - KEY ENDPOINT
- Required role: any authenticated user
- Query params: none
- Success 200:

```json
  "success": true,
  "data": {

    "username": "jdoe",
    "displayName": "Jane Doe",
    "role": "host",
    "assignedStationIds": ["stn_012"],
    "recoveryCodesRemaining": 3,
    "createdAt": "2025-06-15T10:00:00Z",
    "lastLoginAt": "2026-04-03T08:14:22Z"
  }
}
```

**POST /api/auth/recovery-codes (Lines 285-316)**
- Required role: any authenticated user
- Success 200: { codes: [ ... ], generatedAt, warning }
- Notable: All previously unused codes invalidated when new ones generated; audit logs code regeneration

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue';

const apiTarget = process.env.API_PROXY_TARGET || 'https://localhost:3443';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue()],
  server: {
    host: '0.0.0.0',
    port: 8443,
    proxy: {
      '/api': {
        target: apiTarget,
        changeOrigin: true,
        secure: false
      }
    }
  }
});
```

**Key Points:**
- Dev server listens on 0.0.0.0:8443 (all interfaces)
- Proxy `/api` requests to backend (default https://localhost:3443)
- Environment variable: `API_PROXY_TARGET` to override backend URL
- `secure: false` allows self-signed certificates in development
- `changeOrigin: true` rewrites Host header

### 2. docs/design.md (Full Content - 907 lines)

**Location**: `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/docs/design.md`

#### **Key Sections**:

**Section 2: Architecture**
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router (history mode), Pinia stores (authStore, scheduleStore, inventoryStore, searchStore), IndexedDB for hot-search caching, fetch API with Bearer token auth
- **Backend**: Koa middleware layers (tlsEnforce, requestId, rateLimiter, sessionAuth, roleGuard, errorHandler), routes (/api/auth, /schedules, /inventory, /stations, /admin, /search), services (business logic), models (knex-based data access)
- **Database**: MySQL 8 on-site, binary logging enabled for point-in-time recovery
- **Docker Compose**: Frontend (nginx + SPA + TLS), Backend (Node.js Koa), Database (MySQL 8 + persistent volume), Backup sidecar

**Section 3: Data Model (Comprehensive)**

**Identity & Access Tables**:
- `users`: id, username (unique, case-insensitive), display_name, password_hash (bcrypt), phone (masked to last 4 except Platform Ops), role_id, is_active, enrolled_at, created_at, updated_at
- `roles`: id, name (guest, host, platform_ops)
- `permissions`: id, role_id, action (e.g., schedule:draft), scope (own_station, cross_site, public)
- `user_station_scopes`: id, user_id, station_id (Hosts assigned one or more stations; Platform Ops have implicit cross-site access)

**Session & Device Security Tables**:
- `sessions`: id, user_id, token_hash (SHA-256), device_fingerprint, created_at, last_active_at, expires_at (30 days), is_revoked
- `trusted_devices`: id, user_id, device_fingerprint, label, trusted_at
- `recovery_codes`: id, user_id, code_hash (bcrypt), is_used, used_at, created_at
- `login_attempts`: id, username, ip_address, device_fingerprint, success, attempted_at
- `lockouts`: id, user_id (nullable), ip_address, reason, locked_at, expires_at, cleared_by, cleared_at

**Stations Tables**:
- `stations`: id, code (unique), name, normalized_name, timezone (IANA), is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `station_aliases`: id, station_id, alias, normalized_alias

**Trainsets Table**:
- `trainsets`: id, code (unique), description, total_seats, is_active, created_at, updated_at

**Schedules & Versioning Tables**:
- `schedules`: id, train_number, trainset_id, origin_station, dest_station, active_version, created_by, created_at, updated_at
- `schedule_versions`: id, schedule_id, version_number (monotonic per schedule), status (draft, pending_approval, published, archived), effective_from, effective_until, created_by, created_at, published_at, archived_at
- `schedule_stops`: id, version_id, station_id, stop_sequence, arrival_time, departure_time, dwell_minutes
- `seat_classes`: id, version_id, name (Economy, Business, First), capacity (1-500), price ($1-$999)

**Approval & Rollback Tables**:
- `approval_requests`: id, version_id, requested_by (Host), reviewed_by (Platform Ops, nullable), status (pending, approved, rejected), comment, requested_at, reviewed_at
- `rollback_history`: id, schedule_id, from_version_id, to_version_id (newly created), source_version_id (historical), rolled_back_by, reason, created_at

**Inventory Tables**:
- `inventory_items`: id, station_id, sku (unique per station), description, unit_of_measure, tracking_mode (none, batch, serial), reorder_point, unit_cost, is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `inventory_balances`: id, item_id, on_hand (cached from ledger), as_of (recomputation timestamp) — materialized cache
- `inventory_movements`: id, item_id, type (receiving, shipping, material_return, customer_return, adjustment), quantity (positive inbound, negative outbound), unit_cost, reference_number, notes, performed_by, created_at
- `stock_counts`: id, station_id, status (in_progress, completed, cancelled), started_by, started_at, completed_at
- `count_lines`: id, count_id, item_id, expected_qty, counted_qty, variance_qty, variance_cost, resolved
- `batch_records`: id, movement_id, batch_number, expiry_date, quantity
- `serial_records`: id, movement_id, serial_number (unique per item), status (in_stock, shipped, returned, disposed)

**Backup & Recovery Tables**:
- `backups`: id, type (full, incremental), file_path, file_size, checksum (SHA-256), started_at, completed_at, status (running, completed, failed), error_message
- `restore_drills`: id, backup_id, target_schema (scratch schema name), started_at, completed_at, success, row_count_match, report (JSON), performed_by

**Data Quality Tables**:
- `data_quality_issues`: id, table_name, record_id, field_name, severity (critical, high, medium, low), rule_name, description, owner_id, due_date, status (open, in_progress, resolved, wont_fix), resolution, detected_at, resolved_at
- `data_quality_reports`: id, report_date (one per day), total_issues, critical_count, high_count, tables_scanned, records_scanned, pass_rate, report_payload (JSON), generated_at

**Audit Logs Table** (append-only):
- `audit_logs`: id, user_id (nullable), action, table_name, record_id, old_values (JSON), new_values (JSON), ip_address, session_id, created_at

**Section 4: Security Design**

- **Authentication**: Local credentials only (no SSO/LDAP/OAuth). Passwords hashed with bcrypt (cost 12). No plaintext transmission.
- **Session Management**:
  - Token format: 256-bit cryptographically random, stored as SHA-256 hash
  - Inactivity timeout: 8 hours since last authenticated request
  - Hard expiry: 30 days from session creation
  - Max active sessions: 2 per user (Platform Ops can override)
  - Token transport: HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=strict cookie (Bearer header as fallback for API tools)
  - Third session auto-revokes oldest if limit reached

- **Device Fingerprinting**: Browser user-agent + OS identifier + machine key (random UUID in localStorage). Known device = normal login. New device = recovery code challenge.

- **Recovery Codes**: 10 single-use alphanumeric codes generated at enrollment. Stored as bcrypt hash. Displayed once, never again. Single-use, consumed on verification.

- **Progressive Lockout**:
  - 5 failed attempts in 10 min → 15-min account lock
  - 3 lockouts in 24 hours → manual admin reset required
  - Per-user and per-IP evaluation

- **Rate Limiting**: `/api/auth/login` endpoint uses token-bucket algorithm: 10-request bucket, 1 req per 3 seconds, scoped per IP, returns 429 with Retry-After header

- **Row-Level Data Scope**: Hosts see only their assigned stations. Platform Ops see cross-site. Guests see only published schedules via public search.

- **Sensitive Field Masking**: user.phone masked to last 4 digits except for Platform Ops (who see full value)

- **TLS Configuration**: Admins provide cert/key via env vars. Dev uses self-signed certs. Backend rejects plain HTTP. Min TLS 1.2.

**Section 5: Guest Search Design**

- **Search Interface**: Origin (station autocomplete), Destination, Date (MM/DD/YYYY), Seat class (multi-select)
- **Sort Options**: Departure time (default), Trip duration, Price (cheapest available)
- **Fuzzy Matching**: 3-tier strategy — exact match on normalized_name/alias → prefix match → Levenshtein distance (threshold ≤2)
- **Hot Search Cache (IndexedDB)**: Cache key = normalized query string. TTL = 1 hour. Max 200 entries (LRU eviction). Backend sends Last-Modified header; frontend sends If-Modified-Since. 304 Not Modified = cache valid.
- **Performance Targets**:
  - Cache hit (IndexedDB): <50ms
  - Cache miss, warm server: <200ms
  - Cold server start: <500ms
- **Composite Index**: (origin_station, dest_station, status, effective_from) on schedule_versions table

**Section 6: Schedule Management**

- **Draft Workspace**: Max one draft version per schedule. Private to creating Host until submitted.
- **Pre-Publish Validation Checklist**:
  - Min stops: 1 intermediate (origin + destination + 1)
  - Time sequence: Each stop's departure ≥ arrival; each arrival > previous departure
  - Capacity: 1-500
  - Price: $1.00-$999.00
  - Trainset availability: No overlapping published schedules for same time window
- **Version Comparison**: Side-by-side diff view with color coding (green = added, red = removed, amber = modified). Summary stats.
- **Approval Flow**: Draft → pending_approval (submitted by Host) → approved (Platform Ops, becomes published) or rejected (mandatory comment, reverts to draft)
- **Rollback**: Platform Ops can roll back to any published version. Historical version cloned to new row, status = published, previous archived. Full history preserved.
- **Active Version Display**: Labeled with "LIVE" badge, effective-from timestamp, version number, publisher

**Section 7: Inventory**

- **Movement Types**: receiving, shipping, material_return, customer_return, stock_count, adjustment
- **Ledger-Based Balances**: Balance(item, t) = SUM(movements.quantity) WHERE item_id=item AND created_at≤t. `inventory_balances` is materialized cache, recomputed after each movement and every 15 minutes.
- **No-Negative-On-Hand Rule**: Movements rejected if would bring on_hand below zero, except `adjustment` (which corrects discrepancies). Transactional lock on `inventory_balances` row prevents race conditions.
- **Reorder Alerts**: When balance ≤ reorder_point, item highlighted. Default reorder_point = 20 (configurable per item).
- **Variance Alerts During Counts**:
  - Quantity variance > 2% of expected
  - Cost variance > $50.00 extended cost
  - Flagged variances require review before count completion
- **Batch and Serial Tracking**: tracking_mode (none, batch, serial) set at item creation, immutable after first movement. `batch_records` (batch_number, expiry_date). `serial_records` (serial_number, status).

**Section 8: Backup & Recovery**

- **Schedule**: Full backup nightly at 02:00 (mysqldump with --single-transaction --routines --triggers). Incremental every 15 min (binary log archival).
- **Target**: Admin-designated removable drive path (`BACKUP_TARGET_PATH`). Files timestamped (railops_full_20260403_020000.sql.gz). gzip compressed. SHA-256 checksum.
- **Retention**: Full backups 90 days. Binary logs 30 days. Pruned during nightly run.
- **Quarterly Restore Drills**: Select recent backup → restore to scratch schema (e.g., railops_drill_20260403) → compare row counts → create `restore_drills` record → drop scratch schema. Logged in audit trail. Platform Ops reminded if no drill in current quarter.

**Section 9: Data Quality & Lineage**

- **Write-Time Validations**: Completeness, uniqueness, freshness, referential integrity, domain (ranges).
- **Daily Quality Reports**: Job runs 05:00 daily. Scans for missing fields, orphaned FKs, stale records, duplicates, inventory cache drift. Results in `data_quality_reports` and `data_quality_issues`.
- **Issue Tracking**: Severity (critical, high, medium, low), owner (nullable, required for critical/high), due_date, status, resolution.
- **Point-in-Time Diff and Replay**: Using `audit_logs` JSON snapshots. Select two timestamps, view changes. Replay mutations chronologically.
- **Corrective Action Documentation**: Resolver must document what was wrong, what was done, process changes needed.

**Appendix A: Key API Endpoints**
- Authentication endpoints (login, logout, device-challenge)
- Search endpoint (public guest search)
- Schedule endpoints (CRUD, versioning, approval, rejection, rollback)
- Inventory endpoints (items, movements, counts)
- Admin endpoints (backups, data-quality, audit-logs)

- Pagination: `page` (1-based, default 1), `pageSize` (default 25, max 100)

#### **Authentication & Sessions Section** (Lines 114-316):

**POST /api/auth/login** (required role: none/public)
- Request: `username` (3-64 alphanumeric+underscore), `password` (8-128 chars), `deviceFingerprint` (non-empty)
- Success (200): Returns `{ token, expiresAt, user: { id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds } }`
- Errors:
  - 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS (wrong username/password)
  - 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED (5 failures in 10 min, locked 15 min)
  - 403 DEVICE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED (unknown device, response includes sessionToken)
  - 401 ENROLLMENT_REQUIRED (user has no recovery codes)
- Notable: Failed attempts counted. Auto-renewed session via activity. Audit log records success/failure with IP and fingerprint.

**POST /api/auth/logout** (required role: any authenticated)
- Request body: none
- Success (200): `{ message: "Session invalidated." }`
- Error: 401 UNAUTHENTICATED

**POST /api/auth/verify-device** (required role: none, requires valid sessionToken from prior login)
- Request: `sessionToken` (from login response), `code` (8-char alphanumeric recovery code), `deviceFingerprint`
- Success (200): `{ success: true, token, user: {...} }`
- Errors:
  - 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (missing fields)
  - 401 VERIFICATION_FAILED (invalid token or code)
- Notable: Single-use codes. Device added to trusted_devices. Pending verification session deleted.

**GET /api/auth/me** (required role: any authenticated)
- Success (200):

```json
}
```

**POST /api/auth/recovery-codes** (required role: any authenticated)

- Success (200): `{ codes: [10 codes], generatedAt, warning: "Store these codes securely..." }`

## SUMMARY

This comprehensive repository contains a complete offline-first rail operations management suite with:

- **221-line README** with full setup, testing, and deployment instructions
- **907-line Design Document** covering architecture, data model (40+ tables), security design, guest search, schedule management, inventory, backup/recovery, and data quality
- **3702-line API Specification** detailing 50+ REST endpoints with full request/response contracts, error codes, role-based access, and validation rules
- **Vite-based Frontend** (Vue 3 + Pinia + Router) with hot-search IndexedDB caching, dev server on 8443 proxying `/api` to HTTPS backend
- **11 API Test Files** (~94 tests) covering auth, authorization, trips, schedules, inventory, users, stations, backups, data quality, and security regression tests
- **6 Unit Test Files** (~50 tests) covering auth middleware, session lifecycle, validators, fuzzy matching, PII masking, and backup path validation

Key architectural highlights:
- **TLS mandatory** in all non-test modes
- **Session management**: 2 concurrent sessions per user, 30-day hard expiry, 8-hour inactivity timeout
- **Device fingerprinting + recovery codes** for new device verification
- **Progressive lockout** after 5 failed attempts in 10 min
- **Role-based access control** (Guest, Host, Platform Ops) with row-level station scoping for Hosts
- **Audit trail** on all mutations (append-only audit_logs table)
- **Ledger-based inventory** with no-negative-on-hand rule and materialized balance cache
- **Schedule versioning** with draft workspace, pre-publish validation, approval flow, rollback capability
- **Nightly full + 15-min incremental backups** with quarterly restore drills
- **Daily data quality reports** with issue tracking and point-in-time audit replay

All files are thoroughly documented with line-by-line accuracy.

## COMPREHENSIVE REPOSITORY EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. README.md (Full Content - 221 lines)

**Location**: `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/README.md`

The README provides:
- **System Overview**: RailOps is a self-contained, offline-first web application for rail operators. Uses Vue 3 + Pinia + Router frontend, Koa backend, MySQL 8 database, Docker Compose orchestration.
- **Architecture Table**: Frontend (Vue 3 + Pinia + Router), Backend (Koa, Node 18+), Database (MySQL 8.0), Runtime (Docker Compose)
- **Key Security Note**: HTTPS required in production. HTTP only allowed when `NODE_ENV=test` or `SECURITY_MODE=test`
- **Roles**: Guest (read-only), Host (station-scoped), Platform Operations (full access)
- **Quick Start**: Generate TLS certs → Docker Compose up → Access at http://localhost:8443
- **Manual Setup**: Instructions for local development without Docker (requires MySQL, TLS certs, Node.js)
- **Default Credentials**:
  - admin / admin123 (Platform Operations)
  - host1 / host123 (Host)
- **Environment Variables**: MYSQL_DATABASE, DB_HOST, DB_PASSWORD, SESSION_SECRET, TLS_CERT_PATH, TLS_KEY_PATH, BACKUP_PATH
- **Scripts**: generate-certs.sh, seed-dev.sh, backup.sh, restore-drill.sh, run_tests.sh, benchmark-search.sh
- **Performance Target**: Hot searches must return in <200ms (via IndexedDB cache)
- **Testing Framework**:
  - Unit tests (50 tests, no backend needed)
  - Frontend tests (Vitest)
  - API tests (94 tests, requires running backend)
  - Run all: `./run_tests.sh`
- **Project Structure**: Clearly documented directory layout for frontend/, backend/, unit_tests/, API_tests/, docs/, scripts/

---

### 2. docs/design.md (Full Content - 907 lines)

**Location**: `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/docs/design.md`

#### **Key Sections**:

**Section 2: Architecture**
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router (history mode), Pinia stores (authStore, scheduleStore, inventoryStore, searchStore), IndexedDB for hot-search caching, fetch API with Bearer token auth
- **Backend**: Koa middleware layers (tlsEnforce, requestId, rateLimiter, sessionAuth, roleGuard, errorHandler), routes (/api/auth, /schedules, /inventory, /stations, /admin, /search), services (business logic), models (knex-based data access)
- **Database**: MySQL 8 on-site, binary logging enabled for point-in-time recovery
- **Docker Compose**: Frontend (nginx + SPA + TLS), Backend (Node.js Koa), Database (MySQL 8 + persistent volume), Backup sidecar

**Section 3: Data Model (Comprehensive)**

**Identity & Access Tables**:
- `users`: id, username (unique, case-insensitive), display_name, password_hash (bcrypt), phone (masked to last 4 except Platform Ops), role_id, is_active, enrolled_at, created_at, updated_at
- `roles`: id, name (guest, host, platform_ops)
- `permissions`: id, role_id, action (e.g., schedule:draft), scope (own_station, cross_site, public)
- `user_station_scopes`: id, user_id, station_id (Hosts assigned one or more stations; Platform Ops have implicit cross-site access)

**Session & Device Security Tables**:
- `sessions`: id, user_id, token_hash (SHA-256), device_fingerprint, created_at, last_active_at, expires_at (30 days), is_revoked
- `trusted_devices`: id, user_id, device_fingerprint, label, trusted_at
- `recovery_codes`: id, user_id, code_hash (bcrypt), is_used, used_at, created_at
- `login_attempts`: id, username, ip_address, device_fingerprint, success, attempted_at
- `lockouts`: id, user_id (nullable), ip_address, reason, locked_at, expires_at, cleared_by, cleared_at

**Stations Tables**:
- `stations`: id, code (unique), name, normalized_name, timezone (IANA), is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `station_aliases`: id, station_id, alias, normalized_alias

**Trainsets Table**:
- `trainsets`: id, code (unique), description, total_seats, is_active, created_at, updated_at

**Schedules & Versioning Tables**:
- `schedules`: id, train_number, trainset_id, origin_station, dest_station, active_version, created_by, created_at, updated_at
- `schedule_versions`: id, schedule_id, version_number (monotonic per schedule), status (draft, pending_approval, published, archived), effective_from, effective_until, created_by, created_at, published_at, archived_at
- `schedule_stops`: id, version_id, station_id, stop_sequence, arrival_time, departure_time, dwell_minutes
- `seat_classes`: id, version_id, name (Economy, Business, First), capacity (1-500), price ($1-$999)

**Approval & Rollback Tables**:
- `approval_requests`: id, version_id, requested_by (Host), reviewed_by (Platform Ops, nullable), status (pending, approved, rejected), comment, requested_at, reviewed_at
- `rollback_history`: id, schedule_id, from_version_id, to_version_id (newly created), source_version_id (historical), rolled_back_by, reason, created_at

**Inventory Tables**:
- `inventory_items`: id, station_id, sku (unique per station), description, unit_of_measure, tracking_mode (none, batch, serial), reorder_point, unit_cost, is_active, created_at, updated_at
- `inventory_balances`: id, item_id, on_hand (cached from ledger), as_of (recomputation timestamp) — materialized cache
- `inventory_movements`: id, item_id, type (receiving, shipping, material_return, customer_return, adjustment), quantity (positive inbound, negative outbound), unit_cost, reference_number, notes, performed_by, created_at
- `stock_counts`: id, station_id, status (in_progress, completed, cancelled), started_by, started_at, completed_at
- `count_lines`: id, count_id, item_id, expected_qty, counted_qty, variance_qty, variance_cost, resolved
- `batch_records`: id, movement_id, batch_number, expiry_date, quantity
- `serial_records`: id, movement_id, serial_number (unique per item), status (in_stock, shipped, returned, disposed)

**Backup & Recovery Tables**:
- `backups`: id, type (full, incremental), file_path, file_size, checksum (SHA-256), started_at, completed_at, status (running, completed, failed), error_message
- `restore_drills`: id, backup_id, target_schema (scratch schema name), started_at, completed_at, success, row_count_match, report (JSON), performed_by

**Data Quality Tables**:
- `data_quality_issues`: id, table_name, record_id, field_name, severity (critical, high, medium, low), rule_name, description, owner_id, due_date, status (open, in_progress, resolved, wont_fix), resolution, detected_at, resolved_at
- `data_quality_reports`: id, report_date (one per day), total_issues, critical_count, high_count, tables_scanned, records_scanned, pass_rate, report_payload (JSON), generated_at

**Audit Logs Table** (append-only):
- `audit_logs`: id, user_id (nullable), action, table_name, record_id, old_values (JSON), new_values (JSON), ip_address, session_id, created_at

**Section 4: Security Design**

- **Authentication**: Local credentials only (no SSO/LDAP/OAuth). Passwords hashed with bcrypt (cost 12). No plaintext transmission.
- **Session Management**:
  - Token format: 256-bit cryptographically random, stored as SHA-256 hash
  - Inactivity timeout: 8 hours since last authenticated request
  - Hard expiry: 30 days from session creation
  - Max active sessions: 2 per user (Platform Ops can override)
  - Token transport: HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=strict cookie (Bearer header as fallback for API tools)
  - Third session auto-revokes oldest if limit reached

- **Device Fingerprinting**: Browser user-agent + OS identifier + machine key (random UUID in localStorage). Known device = normal login. New device = recovery code challenge.

- **Recovery Codes**: 10 single-use alphanumeric codes generated at enrollment. Stored as bcrypt hash. Displayed once, never again. Single-use, consumed on verification.

- **Progressive Lockout**:
  - 5 failed attempts in 10 min → 15-min account lock
  - 3 lockouts in 24 hours → manual admin reset required
  - Per-user and per-IP evaluation

- **Rate Limiting**: `/api/auth/login` endpoint uses token-bucket algorithm: 10-request bucket, 1 req per 3 seconds, scoped per IP, returns 429 with Retry-After header

- **Row-Level Data Scope**: Hosts see only their assigned stations. Platform Ops see cross-site. Guests see only published schedules via public search.

- **Sensitive Field Masking**: user.phone masked to last 4 digits except for Platform Ops (who see full value)

- **TLS Configuration**: Admins provide cert/key via env vars. Dev uses self-signed certs. Backend rejects plain HTTP. Min TLS 1.2.

**Section 5: Guest Search Design**

- **Search Interface**: Origin (station autocomplete), Destination, Date (MM/DD/YYYY), Seat class (multi-select)
- **Sort Options**: Departure time (default), Trip duration, Price (cheapest available)
- **Fuzzy Matching**: 3-tier strategy — exact match on normalized_name/alias → prefix match → Levenshtein distance (threshold ≤2)
- **Hot Search Cache (IndexedDB)**: Cache key = normalized query string. TTL = 1 hour. Max 200 entries (LRU eviction). Backend sends Last-Modified header; frontend sends If-Modified-Since. 304 Not Modified = cache valid.
- **Performance Targets**:
  - Cache hit (IndexedDB): <50ms
  - Cache miss, warm server: <200ms
  - Cold server start: <500ms
- **Composite Index**: (origin_station, dest_station, status, effective_from) on schedule_versions table

**Section 6: Schedule Management**

- **Draft Workspace**: Max one draft version per schedule. Private to creating Host until submitted.
- **Pre-Publish Validation Checklist**:
  - Min stops: 1 intermediate (origin + destination + 1)
  - Time sequence: Each stop's departure ≥ arrival; each arrival > previous departure
  - Capacity: 1-500
  - Price: $1.00-$999.00
  - Trainset availability: No overlapping published schedules for same time window
- **Version Comparison**: Side-by-side diff view with color coding (green = added, red = removed, amber = modified). Summary stats.
- **Approval Flow**: Draft → pending_approval (submitted by Host) → approved (Platform Ops, becomes published) or rejected (mandatory comment, reverts to draft)
- **Rollback**: Platform Ops can roll back to any published version. Historical version cloned to new row, status = published, previous archived. Full history preserved.
- **Active Version Display**: Labeled with "LIVE" badge, effective-from timestamp, version number, publisher

**Section 7: Inventory**

- **Movement Types**: receiving, shipping, material_return, customer_return, stock_count, adjustment
- **Ledger-Based Balances**: Balance(item, t) = SUM(movements.quantity) WHERE item_id=item AND created_at≤t. `inventory_balances` is materialized cache, recomputed after each movement and every 15 minutes.
- **No-Negative-On-Hand Rule**: Movements rejected if would bring on_hand below zero, except `adjustment` (which corrects discrepancies). Transactional lock on `inventory_balances` row prevents race conditions.
- **Reorder Alerts**: When balance ≤ reorder_point, item highlighted. Default reorder_point = 20 (configurable per item).
- **Variance Alerts During Counts**:
  - Quantity variance > 2% of expected
  - Cost variance > $50.00 extended cost
  - Flagged variances require review before count completion
- **Batch and Serial Tracking**: tracking_mode (none, batch, serial) set at item creation, immutable after first movement. `batch_records` (batch_number, expiry_date). `serial_records` (serial_number, status).

**Section 8: Backup & Recovery**

- **Schedule**: Full backup nightly at 02:00 (mysqldump with --single-transaction --routines --triggers). Incremental every 15 min (binary log archival).
- **Target**: Admin-designated removable drive path (`BACKUP_TARGET_PATH`). Files timestamped (railops_full_20260403_020000.sql.gz). gzip compressed. SHA-256 checksum.
- **Retention**: Full backups 90 days. Binary logs 30 days. Pruned during nightly run.
- **Quarterly Restore Drills**: Select recent backup → restore to scratch schema (e.g., railops_drill_20260403) → compare row counts → create `restore_drills` record → drop scratch schema. Logged in audit trail. Platform Ops reminded if no drill in current quarter.

**Section 9: Data Quality & Lineage**

- **Write-Time Validations**: Completeness, uniqueness, freshness, referential integrity, domain (ranges).
- **Daily Quality Reports**: Job runs 05:00 daily. Scans for missing fields, orphaned FKs, stale records, duplicates, inventory cache drift. Results in `data_quality_reports` and `data_quality_issues`.
- **Issue Tracking**: Severity (critical, high, medium, low), owner (nullable, required for critical/high), due_date, status, resolution.
- **Point-in-Time Diff and Replay**: Using `audit_logs` JSON snapshots. Select two timestamps, view changes. Replay mutations chronologically.
- **Corrective Action Documentation**: Resolver must document what was wrong, what was done, process changes needed.

**Appendix A: Key API Endpoints**
- Authentication endpoints (login, logout, device-challenge)
- Search endpoint (public guest search)
- Schedule endpoints (CRUD, versioning, approval, rejection, rollback)
- Inventory endpoints (items, movements, counts)
- Admin endpoints (backups, data-quality, audit-logs)

---

### 3. docs/api-spec.md (Full Content - 3702 lines)

**Location**: `/Users/aimanmengesha/Desktop/eagle point/Slopering/w2t12/repo/docs/api-spec.md`

#### **Comprehensive API Specification Structure**:

**Conventions Section**:
- Roles: `guest`, `host`, `platform_ops`
- Standard success response format with `{ success: true, data: {...} }`
- List endpoints wrap results in `{ results: [...], total: X, page: X, pageSize: X }`
- Error response format: `{ success: false, error: { code, message, details } }`
- Common HTTP status codes: 400 (VALIDATION_ERROR), 401 (UNAUTHENTICATED), 403 (FORBIDDEN), 404 (NOT_FOUND), 409 (CONFLICT), 423 (ACCOUNT_LOCKED), 429 (RATE_LIMITED), 500 (INTERNAL_ERROR)
- Pagination: `page` (1-based, default 1), `pageSize` (default 25, max 100)
- Authentication Header: `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>`
- Audit Logging: All state-changing requests (POST, PATCH, DELETE) automatically logged

#### **Authentication & Sessions Section** (Lines 114-316):

**POST /api/auth/login** (required role: none/public)
- Request: `username` (3-64 alphanumeric+underscore), `password` (8-128 chars), `deviceFingerprint` (non-empty)
- Success (200): Returns `{ token, expiresAt, user: { id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds } }`
- Errors:
  - 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS (wrong username/password)
  - 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED (5 failures in 10 min, locked 15 min)
  - 403 DEVICE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED (unknown device, response includes sessionToken)
  - 401 ENROLLMENT_REQUIRED (user has no recovery codes)
- Notable: Failed attempts counted. Auto-renewed session via activity. Audit log records success/failure with IP and fingerprint.

**POST /api/auth/logout** (required role: any authenticated)
- Request body: none
- Success (200): `{ message: "Session invalidated." }`
- Error: 401 UNAUTHENTICATED

**POST /api/auth/verify-device** (required role: none, requires valid sessionToken from prior login)
- Request: `sessionToken` (from login response), `code` (8-char alphanumeric recovery code), `deviceFingerprint`
- Success (200): `{ success: true, token, user: {...} }`
- Errors:
  - 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (missing fields)
  - 401 VERIFICATION_FAILED (invalid token or code)
- Notable: Single-use codes. Device added to trusted_devices. Pending verification session deleted.

**GET /api/auth/me** (required role: any authenticated)
- Success (200):
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "usr_001",
    "username": "jdoe",
    "displayName": "Jane Doe",
    "role": "host",
    "assignedStationIds": ["stn_012"],
    "recoveryCodesRemaining": 3,
    "createdAt": "2025-06-15T10:00:00Z",
    "lastLoginAt": "2026-04-03T08:14:22Z"
  }
}
```

**POST /api/auth/recovery-codes** (required role: any authenticated)
- Request body: none
- Success (200): `{ codes: [10 codes], generatedAt, warning: "Store these codes securely..." }`
- Notable: All previously unused codes invalidated. Audit log records generation.

#### **Users & Admin Section** (Lines 319-660):

**GET /api/users** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Query params: `page`, `pageSize`, `role`, `q` (search by username/display_name), `locked`
- Success: Paginated results with user objects (id, username, displayName, role, assignedStationIds, locked, createdAt, lastLoginAt)

**POST /api/users** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `username` (3-64, alphanumeric+underscore, unique), `displayName` (1-128), `password` (8-128, complexity rules), `role` (host or platform_ops), `assignedStationIds` (optional)
- Success (201): User object with id, createdAt
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (duplicate username)

**PATCH /api/users/:id** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: All fields optional — `displayName`, `role`, `assignedStationIds`
- Success (200): Updated user object
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**GET /api/users/:id/sessions** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Paginated sessions with sessionId, deviceFingerprint, ipAddress, createdAt, expiresAt, lastActivityAt

**DELETE /api/users/:id/sessions/:sessionId** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): `{ message: "Session revoked." }`
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**POST /api/users/:id/session-exception** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `maxSessions` (2-10), `expiresAt` (ISO 8601 future), `reason` (1-500 chars)
- Success (200): `{ userId, maxSessions, expiresAt, grantedBy, grantedAt }`

**POST /api/users/:id/unlock** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request body: none
- Success (200): `{ userId, locked: false, unlockedBy, unlockedAt }`
- Errors: 404 NOT_FOUND, 409 CONFLICT (not locked)
- Notable: Resets failed-login counter. Audit log records unlock.

**POST /api/users/:id/reset-password** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `newPassword` (8-128, complexity rules)
- Success (200): `{ userId, mustChangePassword: true, resetBy, resetAt }`
- Notable: All active sessions invalidated. User required to change password on next login. Audit log records reset.

#### **Stations Section** (Lines 661-847):

**GET /api/stations** (required role: any authenticated)
- Query params: `page`, `pageSize`, `q` (fuzzy search by name/code, trigram similarity)
- Success (200): Paginated stations with id, code, name, timezone, platforms, coordinates (lat/lng), active, createdAt, updatedAt

**GET /api/stations/:id** (required role: any authenticated)
- Success (200): Full station detail including assignedHosts array (id, displayName)
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

**POST /api/stations** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `code` (2-10, uppercase, unique), `name` (1-200, unique), `timezone` (IANA), `platforms` (1-100), `coordinates` (optional, {lat, lng})
- Success (201): Station object with id, createdAt, updatedAt
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (duplicate code/name)

**PATCH /api/stations/:id** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: All optional — `name`, `timezone`, `platforms`, `coordinates`, `active` (boolean)
- Success (200): Updated station object
- Error: 404 NOT_FOUND

#### **Trainsets Section** (Lines 850-950+):

**GET /api/trainsets** (required role: any authenticated)
- Query params: `page`, `pageSize`, `status` (active, maintenance, retired)
- Success (200): Paginated trainsets with id, code, name, type (electric, diesel, hybrid), cars, totalSeats, status, homeStationId, createdAt, updatedAt

**POST /api/trainsets** (required role: `host` or `platform_ops`)
- Request: `code` (2-20, unique), `name` (1-200), `type` (electric, diesel, hybrid), `cars` (1-30), `totalSeats` (1-2000), `status` (optional, default active), `homeStationId` (required)
- Success (201): Trainset object with id, createdAt, updatedAt
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 409 CONFLICT (duplicate code)

**PATCH /api/trainsets/:id** (required role: `host` or `platform_ops`)
- Request: All optional — `name`, `type`, `cars`, `totalSeats`, `status`, `homeStationId`
- Success (200): Updated trainset object

#### **Trip Search (Guest)** Section:

**GET /api/trips/search** (required role: none/guest)
- Query params: `origin` (required), `destination` (required), `date` (MM/DD/YYYY, optional), `seatClass` (optional), `sort` (optional: departure, duration, price)
- Success (200): `{ results: [ { routeName, origin: {stationName, departureAt}, destination: {stationName}, seatClasses: [{className, classCode, fare}], durationMinutes } ] }`
- Errors: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (missing origin/destination, invalid date format)

**GET /api/trips/hot-searches** (required role: none/guest)
- Success (200): Array of popular search pairs with origin and destination fields

#### **Schedules** Section:

**GET /api/schedules** (required role: `host`, `platform_ops`)
- Hosts see only their assigned stations; Platform Ops see all
- Success (200): Array of schedule objects

**GET /api/schedules/:id** (required role: authenticated)
- Success (200): Full schedule detail

**GET /api/schedules/:id/versions** (required role: authenticated)
- Success (200): Array of versions with status, effective_from, created_at

**POST /api/schedules** (required role: authenticated)
- Request: `route_name`, `station_id`, `trainset_id`, `stops` (array), `seat_classes` (array)
- Success (200/201): Schedule object with id

**POST /api/schedules/:id/versions/:vid/validate** (required role: authenticated)
- Request body: none
- Success (200): `{ valid: boolean, checks: [...] }` — runs pre-publish validation checklist

**POST /api/schedules/:id/rollback** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `source_version_id` (must be published, not draft), `reason`
- Success (200): New version cloned from source
- Error: 409 CONFLICT (if source is draft)

#### **Backups & Data Quality** Section:

**GET /api/backups** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Paginated backup records with backup_type, file_path, file_size, checksum, started_at, completed_at, status

**GET /api/backups/config** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Backup configuration

**POST /api/backups/run** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `backup_type` (full or incremental)
- Success (200/202/409): 200 = completed sync, 202 = accepted async, 409 = already running
- Error: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (invalid backup_type)

**GET /api/restore-drills** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Paginated drill records

**GET /api/data-quality/issues** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Paginated issue records with severity, status, owner_id, due_date, resolution

**POST /api/data-quality/issues** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Request: `entity_type`, `check_type`, `severity`, `description`
- Success (200/201): Issue object

**GET /api/data-quality/reports** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Success (200): Paginated daily reports with total_issues, critical_count, pass_rate, report_payload (JSON)

**GET /api/audit/logs** (required role: `platform_ops`)
- Query params: `action` (filter by action type), `page`, `pageSize`
- Success (200): Paginated audit entries with user_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_values, new_values, ip_address, created_at
